questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Rapid'.
questions.explanation: Rapid means happening quickly; the closest synonym is Fast.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.explanation: Ancient refers to something very old; its opposite is Modern.

questions.question: Choose the correct spelling.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is Accommodate with double 'c' and double 'm'.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: She ___ going to the market.
questions.explanation: The subject 'She' requires the singular verb 'is'.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.explanation: Joyful means feeling great happiness.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Increase'.
questions.explanation: Reduce means to make smaller, opposite of Increase.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: They ___ playing cricket.
questions.explanation: 'They' is plural, so the correct helping verb is 'are'.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Brave'.
questions.explanation: Bold means showing courage, similar to Brave.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.explanation: Victory means winning; its opposite is Defeat.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: He ___ finished his work.
questions.explanation: 'He' is singular and uses 'has' in present perfect tense.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Begin'.
questions.explanation: Begin and Start have the same meaning.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Rich'.
questions.explanation: Poor is the opposite of Rich.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: I ___ a book yesterday.
questions.explanation: Yesterday indicates past tense, so 'bought' is correct.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Quick'.
questions.explanation: Rapid means happening quickly.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Strong'.
questions.explanation: Weak is the opposite of Strong.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: She ___ to school every day.
questions.explanation: Third person singular requires 'goes'.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Big'.
questions.explanation: Large means big in size.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Hot'.
questions.explanation: Cold is opposite of Hot.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: They ___ finished their lunch.
questions.explanation: Plural subject 'They' takes 'have'.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Smart'.
questions.explanation: Smart means intelligent.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Early'.
questions.explanation: Late is the opposite of Early.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: He ___ playing football.
questions.explanation: Singular subject 'He' takes 'is'.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Angry'.
questions.explanation: Furious means very angry.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Tall'.
questions.explanation: Short is the opposite of Tall.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: We ___ watching a movie.
questions.explanation: 'We' is plural and uses 'are'.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Clean'.
questions.explanation: Pure can mean free from dirt or impurities.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Open'.
questions.explanation: Close is opposite of Open.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: She ___ tea every morning.
questions.explanation: Third person singular requires 'drinks'.

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Fear'.
questions.explanation: Terror means extreme fear.

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Win'.
questions.explanation: Defeat means to lose.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: I ___ finished my homework.
questions.explanation: 'I' uses 'have' in present perfect.
